diive.pkgs.flux.ustarthreshold.UstarThresholdConstantScenarios(series, ustar, swinpot)[source]¶

Check impact of different constant USTAR thresholds on available data

Constant means that the threshold is the same for all data, e.g. the same for all years. …

Methods:
calc(ustarthresholds:list=None, showplot: bool = False, verbose: bool = False):

Creates timeseries of series in after application of different USTAR thresholds given in ustarthresholds.

Properties:
scenariosdf: DataFrame of the timeseries of series in different USTAR

scenarios. Records of series where USTAR was below the respective threshold are set to missing.

Parameters:

  • series (Series)

  • ustar (Series)

  • swinpot (Series)

class diive.pkgs.flux.ustarthreshold.UstarDetectionMPT(df, nee_col, ta_col, ustar_col, ta_n_classes=6, ustar_n_classes=20, season_col=None, n_bootstraps=100, swin_pot_col=None, nighttime_threshold=20, utc_offset=None, lat=None, lon=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

USTAR THRESHOLD DETECTION¶

How thresholds are calculated¶

  • Data are divided into S seasons

  • In each season, data are divided into X air temperature (TA) classes

  • Each air temperature class is divided into Y ustar (USTAR) subclasses (data are binned per subclass)

  • Thresholds are calculated in each TA class

  • Season thresholds per bootstrapping run are calculated from all found TA class thresholds, e.g. the max of thresholds

  • Overall season thresholds are calculated from all season thresholds from all bootstrapping runs, e.g. the median

  • Yearly thresholds are calculated from overall season thresholds

flux_plateau_thres_perc = 95¶
ta_ustar_corr_thres = 0.4¶
flux_plateau_method = 'NEE > 10+10 Higher USTAR Subclasses'¶
thres_class_col = 'USTAR_MPT_THRES_IN_CLASS'¶
thres_season_col = 'USTAR_MPT_THRES_IN_SEASON'¶
thres_year_col = 'USTAR_MPT_THRES_IN_YEAR'¶
bts_run_col = 'BTS_RUN'¶
results_max_bts_col = 'MAX_BTS'¶
results_min_bts_col = 'MIN_BTS'¶
results_median_bts_col = 'MEDIAN_BTS'¶
results_mean_bts_col = 'MEAN_BTS'¶
results_p16_bts_col = 'P16_BTS'¶
results_p84_bts_col = 'P84_BTS'¶
results_bts_runs_col = 'BTS_RUNS'¶
results_bts_results_col = 'BTS_RESULTS'¶
define_bts_season_thres = 'Median of Class Thresholds'¶
define_overall_season_thres = 'Maximum Of Bootstrap Season Thresholds'¶
__init__(df, nee_col, ta_col, ustar_col, ta_n_classes=6, ustar_n_classes=20, season_col=None, n_bootstraps=100, swin_pot_col=None, nighttime_threshold=20, utc_offset=None, lat=None, lon=None)[source]¶
Parameters:

  • df (DataFrame)

  • nee_col (str)

  • ta_col (str)

  • ustar_col (str)

  • ta_n_classes (int)

  • ustar_n_classes (int)

  • n_bootstraps (int)

  • swin_pot_col (str)

  • nighttime_threshold (float)

  • utc_offset (int)

  • lat (float)

  • lon (float)

sample_data(df)[source]¶

Draw samples from data (bootstrapping).

init_results_seasons_df()[source]¶

Create dataframe that contains various threshold results for each season.

init_results_years_df()[source]¶

Create dataframe that contains various threshold results for each year.

run()[source]¶
set_yearly_thresholds(df)[source]¶

Calculate yearly thresholds from season thresholds in full-resolution dataframe.

collect_year_results(yearly_thresholds_df, results_years_df)[source]¶

Calculate different variants of year thresholds and the overall year threshold, based on bts results. Calculations are done afer each bts run.

collect_yearly_thresholds(bts_run)[source]¶
collect_season_results(bts_results_df, season_key, results_df)[source]¶

Calculate different variants of season thresholds and the overall season threshold, based on bts results. Calculations are done after each bts run.

prepare_results_for_collection(df, bts_run)[source]¶

Narrow df to ustar results.

set_season_threshold(season_subclass_avg_df, season_key)[source]¶

Set threshold for current season, based on class thresholds.

Season threshold is calculated from the class thresholds.

detect_class_thresholds(season_subclass_avg_df, ta_class_col, season_key)[source]¶

Calculate ustar thresholds in air temperature classes

Detection is done for each USTAR subclass in each TA class separately for each season.

Analysis is done using the pandas .groupby method. In addition, a MultiIndex DataFrame that contains the subclass averages within each class needs to be accessed. Since the index of the DataFrame is a MultiIndex, it can be accessed via tuples, e.g. SEASON 3 with TA class 0 and with USTAR subclass 4 is accessed with the tuple (3,0,4). Yes, this is a bit tricky, but also very useful, isn’t it.

This analysis could also have been done in an earlier step, but it is useful to have all results in one DataFrame.

ustar_subclass_info(cur_season, cur_class, cur_subclass, cur_flux, cur_ustar, cur_following_mean, nxt_following_mean, nxt_flux, cur_flux_perc, nxt_flux_perc)[source]¶
calculate_subclass_medians(season_df, ta_class_col, ustar_subclass_col, season_key)[source]¶

In each TA class, calculate the TA and FLUX medians in each USTAR subclass.

Create df that contains all the subclass medians per TA class and season.

Args:

season_df: data for season ta_class_col: column name of air temperature ustar_subclass_col: column name of USTAR season_key: season identifier

Returns:

DataFrame with MultiIndex (3 levels)

Parameters:

  • df (DataFrame)

  • nee_col (str)

  • ta_col (str)

  • ustar_col (str)

  • ta_n_classes (int)

  • ustar_n_classes (int)

  • season_col (str)

  • n_bootstraps (int)

  • swin_pot_col (str)

  • nighttime_threshold (float)

  • utc_offset (int)

  • lat (float)

  • lon (float)
